goal: Fix playtest-reported spawn/checkpoint/respawn bugs in isolation first, then add camp enemy variety and menu/title polish, per Chris's explicit sequencing directive.
completed: Diagnosed (3 parallel Explore agents) the root causes behind spawn-near-camp, unfair respawn, and camp skeletons reappearing
Slice A: New Game / Continue flow on the title (game.gd has_save()/resume_requested; player_select.gd two-stage MODE/COUNT flow)
Slice A: replaced flaky 40px Area2D checkpoint triggers with progress-based (furthest area walked past, by world-x) checkpointing; moved camp checkpoint to (108,24), west of the camp's aggro radius

Slice A: fair respawn - Enemy.return_home() resets survivors to their post instead of _reset_area respawning all specs on a partial clear; boss area still fully respawns on death; removed dead respawn_point field
Verified Slice A confirmed by Chris: 'no skeletons on Continue' traced to _apply_resume clearing already-passed areas - expected, not a bug
Slice B: new BombEnemy (stationary, wind-up + AoE detonate + gas VFX, defusable) added to the forest camp; camp is now 3 skeletons + 1 bombschroom instead of 4 identical skeletons
Slice D1: added mouse (hover-highlight + click-activate) as a second input path on the title and pause menus, keyboard untouched
Slice D2: dressed the title screen - dimmed baked-world backdrop slice, UI_Frames NinePatch panel, pixel font, looping campfire critter - reusing existing assets/idioms only
Updated PROJECT.md status after each slice; all work gdparse/gdformat/gdlint-clean and check_symbols.py green throughout
discovered: player_select never called Game.reset_run() - every launch silently auto-resumed the last save, which is why a stale checkpoint could spawn the player next to the camp
player.respawn_point was written in three places but never read anywhere - fully dead code masking the real respawn path (Game.checkpoint -> area center)
_reset_area respawned ALL of an area's enemy specs whenever the area wasn't FULLY cleared, so a partial-clear death repopulated already-killed enemies
Camp skeletons aggro at 110px while the old checkpoint trigger was only 40px - a player could be killed before the checkpoint registered

The base paid Cute Fantasy pack (assets/cute_fantasy/packs/Cute_Fantasy/Cute_Fantasy/) is NOT gdignored (only sub-packs like UI/Christmas/Halloween are), so Bombschroom.png, its gas VFX, and Campfire_Anim.png could be referenced directly with zero asset copying
Bombschroom.png (176x336) is not a clean 32px grid - only row 0 (frames 0-1) reads as a clean idle bob; wind-up/pop are done via code (flash+scale) rather than trusting the messy sheet rows
assets/generated/valley-1-ground.png (3072x768, the baked world) is safe to reference directly and a cropped AtlasTexture region of it makes a usable, on-theme title backdrop with zero new art
decisions: Checkpoints are recorded by player progress (furthest area's road-x walked past), not by trigger volumes - monotonic, immune to trigger-radius/aggro-radius mismatches
On a wipe, only the boss area gets a full fresh respawn; all other areas rearm surviving enemies in place and leave killed ones dead
Camp enemy mix is 3 skeletons + 1 bombschroom (not 2 new types) per Chris's explicit choice, keeping the lone path skeleton unchanged as the teaching encounter
Mouse support is strictly additive (hover-select, click-on-highlighted-to-confirm) - keyboard input paths were not touched or restructured
Title screen reuses the pause-menu's proven Theme+NinePatch(UI_Frames) idiom rather than inventing a new UI pattern
lessons: When multiple 'obviously wrong' bugs are reported together, they're worth diagnosing as one system before patching symptoms - three surface reports (spawn location, respawn destination, enemies reappearing) traced to two root causes (no new-game reset, and re-spawn-on-partial-clear)

Godot .gdignore is per-directory, not tree-wide from a parent - always check whether the SPECIFIC sub-pack folder actually has a .gdignore before assuming a copy into assets/game/ is required; the base pack here didn't, saving several unnecessary asset copies

Before wiring hframes/vframes on an unfamiliar spritesheet, render a gridded contact-sheet probe and inspect visually - Bombschroom.png's nominal dimensions implied a clean grid that wasn't actually clean past row 0

Dead fields (like the old respawn_point) can survive multiple review passes because they compile and 'look' like they're doing something - grep for actual reads, not just writes, when auditing a suspect system
open_questions: Is the (108,24) camp checkpoint cell actually a comfortable, non-cramped spot to respawn on once Chris plays it, or does it need nudging?
Does the bombschroom's balance (22px trigger, 30px blast, 0.6s windup, 3hp) feel fair, or does it need tuning after playtest?
Will the larger centered title text on the new title screen smear like the boss 'Icene' bug (same root cause, not yet fixed) - flagged but unverified
intent: Ended the turn after pushing all three approved build items (Slice B bombschroom, D1 mouse menus, D2 title polish) plus a PROJECT.md status update, with the confirmed Slice A fix as the foundation - handing back to Chris for Windows playtest before starting the next queued batch (Slice C: rocks, flowers, font smear, dash tuning, animal wander, extra boss lines).
next_task: Chris plays the pushed batch on Windows (camp bombschroom feel, mouse menus, new title screen) and reports back; then start Slice C (remove baked procedural ore scatter to fix walk-through/duplicated rocks, fix flower-on-goose + doubled duck, disable pixel-font sub-pixel positioning, shorten dash, animal idle+wander, extra Irene lines).
dont_retry: Don't assume a pack sub-directory is gdignored just because sibling packs are - check for that specific folder's .gdignore before copying assets into assets/game/

Don't wire spritesheet hframes/vframes purely from pixel-dimension division without visually inspecting a gridded probe first - some sheets (Bombschroom.png) aren't clean grids past the first few rows
